summary: Powerful The Other One > Stella Blue sequence with tight second‑set jamming
blurb: The second set erupts with a soaring The Other One → Stella Blue stretch, backed by Jerry’s mature tone and Brent’s lush organ comps. The first set is uneven, but highlights like Bird Song and Uncle John’s Band keep the momentum. Overall a solid spring ’88 show worth revisiting.
review: On March 18 1988 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center the Dead delivered one of those classic late‑80s second‑set surges that fans still talk about. After a lukewarm first set – a mellow opener with Iko Iko and a few dragging numbers – the band launched into an inspired post‑drums stretch. The chanting drums segue into Space builds a cosmic vibe before The Other One unfolds, featuring Jerry’s “wounded‑but‑strong” solo that many reviewers called “hyperspace sci‑fi”. That momentum carries straight into Stella Blue, where Brent’s Hammond organ interplays beautifully with Garcia’s lead work, creating one of the night’s most memorable moments. Highlights also include a bright Bird Song and a tight Uncle John’s Band, while the rare Iko Iko → Man Smart, Woman Smarter transition adds a fun nod to the setlist. Though the first half feels uneven, the second‑set brilliance and strong musicianship make this show a standout snapshot of the band’s spring ’88 form.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are explicitly positive, praising the The Other One > Stella Blue sequence, Jerry’s tone and Brent’s organ work; three others are mixed but contain no negative criticism of performance. The overall sentiment is moderately positive, warranting a +1.0 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard with excellent fidelity; captures the powerful second‑set The Other One > Stella Blue and highlights Brent’s organ and Jerry’s guitar.

key_highlights: The Other One → Stella Blue sequence lifts the entire second set
Jerry’s mature, sci‑fi‑flavored guitar tone and soaring solo on The Other One
Brent’s beautiful Hammond organ comps weave tightly with the guitars

band_performance.Jerry: Shows perfect tone and a mature improvisational touch; his ‘hyperspace sci‑fi’ guitar on The Music Never Stopped and a powerful, wounded‑but‑strong solo on The Other One are repeatedly praised.
band_performance.Bob: Adds creepy, exciting vocal effects on The Other One; occasional criticism of insincere vocals elsewhere but overall his contributions fit the set’s mood.
band_performance.Brent: Delivers beautiful Hammond organ comping with exceptional interplay with Jerry, providing superb keyboard texture throughout the show.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming becomes space‑y and intense during the chanting segment before Space, adding to the second‑set excitement.
band_performance.Billy: Shares in the driving, chant‑filled drum energy that propels the post‑drums improvisations.
